outlets.name: CNN
outlets.headline: Spain bans US military flights as Trump threatens trade war with NATO ally over Iran
outlets.summary: CNN's Day 31 liveblog leads with Spain's unprecedented airspace closure to US military flights — the first formal NATO defiance over the Iran war. Trump responded by threatening trade tariffs against Spain, raising the prospect of a simultaneous NATO trade war.

outlets.name: Washington Post
outlets.headline: Trump declares 'regime change achieved' in Iran — experts warn it's premature
outlets.summary: WaPo scrutinizes Trump's claim in an FT interview that 'regime change has been achieved' in Iran, quoting Iran policy experts who warn the regime remains intact and the claim could harden Iranian resolve against negotiation.

outlets.name: NPR
outlets.headline: Day 31: IDF strikes 120+ targets in Tehran; Haifa oil refinery ablaze as war escalates
outlets.summary: NPR covers the largest single-night Israeli air campaign of the war alongside the Haifa Bazan refinery fire, noting the parallel energy infrastructure targeting: Israel hit Tabriz Petrochemical, Iran hit Haifa Bazan on the same day.

outlets.name: New York Times
outlets.headline: As Trump Talks Regime Change, Iran Fires Back at Haifa Oil Refinery and Kuwait Civilian Plant
outlets.summary: NYT Day 31 coverage highlights the dangerous symmetry: while Trump claims regime change, Iran demonstrated continued strike capability by hitting Israel's biggest oil refinery and killing an Indian worker at a Kuwait civilian plant.

outlets.name: Fox News
outlets.headline: Trump declares regime change achieved in Iran, eyes Kharg Island seizure to end war
outlets.summary: Fox News leads with Trump's FT interview declaring 'regime change achieved' and his threat to seize Kharg Island, framing it as a winning move. IDF's 120-target overnight raid covered as proof of military dominance.

outlets.name: NY Post
outlets.headline: Trump: 'Regime change achieved' in Iran — threatens to take $1 trillion oil island
outlets.summary: NY Post leads with Trump's most aggressive statement yet — claiming regime change and threatening to seize Kharg Island — framing it as maximum pressure working.

outlets.name: Washington Times
outlets.headline: Spain's airspace snub exposes European weakness as Trump wages war on Islamism
outlets.summary: Conservative outlet covers Spain's airspace closure as a failure of European resolve, backing Trump's trade tariff threat against Madrid and calling out the Sanchez government's 'appeasement' of Iran.

outlets.name: Breitbart
outlets.headline: Spain bans US military flights: Trump vows tariffs against NATO ally that chose Iran's side
outlets.summary: Breitbart frames Spain's decision as a betrayal of the Western alliance, cheering Trump's tariff threat and suggesting other NATO members who oppose the war should face similar consequences.

outlets.name: Al Jazeera
outlets.headline: US-Israel war on Iran: What's happening on day 30 of attacks?
outlets.summary: Al Jazeera's comprehensive Day 30 summary covers the Beersheba chemical plant scare, Bandar Khamir port strike, Houthi attacks, and diplomatic paralysis as Pakistan, Turkey, Egypt and Saudi Arabia seek de-escalation.

outlets.name: BBC
outlets.headline: Spain closes airspace to US as NATO cracks over Iran war; IDF hits 120 Tehran targets overnight
outlets.summary: BBC leads with the NATO fracture story — Spain's airspace closure is the top diplomatic development of Day 31. Also covers the IDF's largest single-night bombing run, the Haifa refinery fire, and Indian worker killed in Kuwait.

outlets.name: Reuters
outlets.headline: Israel bombs Tabriz Petrochemical; Iran fires on Haifa refinery and kills Indian worker in Kuwait
outlets.summary: Reuters covers symmetric energy infrastructure strikes: IDF hit Tabriz Petrochemical, Iranian forces fired on Haifa Bazan Oil Refinery. Also reports Indian worker death at Kuwait civilian plant and Trump's 'regime change achieved' claim.

outlets.name: AP
outlets.headline: Trump claims regime change, threatens oil island seizure; Spain defies US as war enters Day 31
outlets.summary: AP covers Trump's escalating rhetoric claiming 'regime change achieved' and Kharg Island seizure threat alongside Spain's historic airspace ban — the first NATO defiance over the Iran war. Indian worker killed in Kuwait adds humanitarian dimension.

outlets.name: Times of Israel
outlets.headline: Haifa Bazan refinery ablaze after Iranian barrage; IDF bombs 120+ Tehran targets overnight
outlets.summary: ToI Day 31 liveblog leads with the Haifa Bazan refinery fire — Israel's largest oil refinery struck for the first time. IDF overnight 120+ target campaign in Tehran and the IDF Alpinist Unit's special operations cross-border raid into Lebanon also covered.

outlets.name: Haaretz
outlets.headline: Haifa oil refinery ablaze as Iran proves strike capability — and Trump claims 'regime change achieved'
outlets.summary: Haaretz juxtaposes Trump's 'regime change achieved' claim against the Haifa Bazan refinery fire, noting the contradiction: if Iranian military capability is destroyed, how is Israel's largest refinery now burning?

outlets.name: Ynet
outlets.headline: Massive fire at Bazan oil refinery in Haifa after Iranian missile hits; 120+ IDF strikes in Tehran
outlets.summary: Ynet covers the Haifa Bazan refinery fire extensively, noting it is the first confirmed hit on a major Israeli energy processing facility. Also covers the IDF Alpinist Unit raid and Spain's airspace closure creating diplomatic complications for Israel.

outlets.name: Press TV
outlets.headline: Araghchi: 'Your actions ooze of desperation' — Iran responds to IDF 120-target Tehran wave
outlets.summary: Iranian state broadcaster leads with FM Araghchi's defiant statement dismissing the IDF's largest overnight bombing campaign as an act of 'desperation,' while framing Iran's Haifa refinery strike and Kuwait operation as legitimate resistance responses.

outlets.name: IRNA
outlets.headline: Iran forces strike Haifa oil refinery; IDF criminally targets Tabriz Petrochemical in civilian attack
outlets.summary: IRNA frames Day 31 as 'symmetric retaliation': Iran's resistance forces hit the Haifa Bazan refinery while condemning IDF strikes on Tabriz Petrochemical as an attack on civilian industrial infrastructure. Spain airspace closure framed as global isolation of US-Israeli aggression.

outlets.name: Tasnim News
outlets.headline: Spain closes airspace to US military, exposing global opposition to Iran war; Trump retaliates with tariff threats
outlets.summary: Tasnim frames Spain's airspace closure as evidence of growing international opposition to US-Israeli aggression, noting Trump's tariff threats prove the US is losing its European allies. Mojtaba Khamenei's thanks to Iraq for support also covered.
